title: Find Median from Data Stream
prompt: Design a data structure that supports `addNum(num)` and `findMedian()` in a streaming fashion. The median is the middle value of an ordered list (or average of two middle values). Inputs are op protocols of the form `[['MedianFinder'], ['addNum', n], ['findMedian'], ...]`; outputs are `null` for ctor/addNum and the current median for findMedian.
hints: Maintain two heaps: a max-heap for the lower half and min-heap for the upper half.
Median = lo.top() if lo bigger, else (lo.top()+hi.top())/2.
pitfalls: Off-by-one when the lower half should always have ≥ size of upper.
Returning integer division for the average.
followups: What if all numbers are in [0, 100]?
What if 99% of numbers are in [0, 100]?
